title: Ontology Map Toolkit — Graph Data
description: The ONLY mandatory input for the Ontology Map Toolkit. Defines nodes and edges of a system graph. The inference engine derives everything else (template, layout, colors, shapes, sizes) from this data alone. cockpit_schema.yaml is optional — it only overrides inferred defaults.

nodes.description: Array of nodes representing system components (services, files, classes, modules, etc.). At least one node is required.

edges.description: Array of edges representing relationships between nodes (imports, calls, contains, depends_on, etc.). Can be empty if the system has no relationships.

metadata.description: Optional metadata about the graph itself (system name, version, generator tool).

Node.description: A single node in the system graph. Only 'id' is required — all other fields are optional and used by the inference engine to make better decisions about template, layout, colors, shapes, and sizing.

id.description: Unique identifier for this node. Used as reference in edges. Must be unique within the graph. Convention: lowercase, alphanumeric + underscores (e.g., 'auth_service', 'user_model').

label.description: Human-readable display name for this node. If omitted, the inference engine derives a label from the id (e.g., 'auth_service' → 'Auth Service').

type.description: Semantic type of this node. Used by the inference engine for template detection (e.g., 'service' → microservices template) and shape mapping (e.g., 'database' → cylinder shape). Examples: 'service', 'file', 'class', 'function', 'module', 'package', 'database', 'queue', 'api', 'config', 'test', 'layer'.

group.description: Category for grouping and coloring. Nodes with the same group get the same color from the categorical palette. Examples: 'backend', 'frontend', 'infra', 'data', 'auth'. If omitted, the inference engine groups by 'type'.

parent.description: ID of the parent node, establishing containment hierarchy. Example: a file node's parent is its package node. Used by the inference engine to create submaps and determine drill-down structure. Must reference an existing node id.

properties.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for this node. Used by the inference engine for sizing (loc, complexity), by the detail panel for display, and by adapters for domain-specific data. All properties are optional.

loc.description: Lines of code. Used by the inference engine for node sizing (log10 scale). Higher LOC = larger node.

complexity.description: Cyclomatic complexity or similar metric. Used by the inference engine for node sizing as fallback when LOC is unavailable.

language.description: Programming language. Used for syntax highlighting in fractal drill-down (F-06). Examples: 'python', 'javascript', 'typescript', 'rust', 'go'.

path.description: File system path relative to project root. Used by fractal drill-down (F-06) to load source code.

description.description: Human-readable description of this node. Displayed in the detail panel.

x.description: Explicit X position in 3D space. If provided, overrides the layout algorithm for this node. Note: the inference engine also checks for x/y/z as top-level node fields (since additionalProperties is true) for compatibility with compact formats.

z.description: Explicit Z position in 3D space. If provided, overrides the layout algorithm for this node.

y.description: Explicit Y position in 3D space (height). If provided, overrides the layout algorithm for this node.

Edge.description: A directed relationship between two nodes. Only 'from' and 'to' are required. Edge type is used by the inference engine for template detection and edge styling.

from.description: ID of the source node. Must reference an existing node id.

to.description: ID of the target node. Must reference an existing node id.

type.description: Semantic type of this edge. Used by the inference engine for template detection (e.g., many 'HTTP' edges → microservices template) and edge styling (color, animation). Examples: 'CONTAINS', 'IMPORTS', 'CALLS', 'DEPENDS_ON', 'HTTP', 'GRPC', 'PUBLISHES', 'SUBSCRIBES', 'GROUNDS', 'INFORMS', 'INSTANTIATES'.

properties.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for this edge.

weight.description: Strength of the relationship. Used by force layout (stronger = closer) and edge styling (thicker). Range: 0.0-1.0.

confidence.description: How certain we are that this relationship exists. Low confidence edges may be rendered with dashed lines. Range: 0.0-1.0.

label.description: Optional label displayed on the edge in the 3D scene.

color.description: Explicit color for this edge. Can be a named color (e.g., 'green', 'blue'), hex (e.g., '#34d399'), or oklch (e.g., 'oklch(70% 0.15 150)'). If omitted, the inference engine assigns color by edge type.

name.description: Name of the system represented by this graph. Displayed as the cockpit title. Examples: 'Engineering Brain', 'Auth Microservices', 'Data Pipeline'.

version.description: Version of this graph snapshot. Useful for time-travel (F-09). Follows semver or date format.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this graph was generated.

generator.description: Tool that produced this graph. Examples: 'ontology-map-toolkit/codebase-ast', 'ontology-map-toolkit/openapi', 'custom'. Informational only.

node_count.description: Total number of nodes. Informational — must match nodes array length.

edge_count.description: Total number of edges. Informational — must match edges array length.
